title: Add evolution log
reflection: Created the evolution log system and daily-evolve workflow for autonomous improvement.
looking_ahead: Event permanence: meteor craters, flood channels

title: Temperament colors
reflection: Replaced single energy-colored pixel with continuous hue from temperament axes.
looking_ahead: Sound envelopes for musical bond moments
Activate spatial hash grid for performance
Event permanence: terrain marks that persist through the cycle

title: Blended bond colors
reflection: Bond lines now blend the temperament colors of both endpoints. Pre-computed mote colors into a Map.
looking_ahead: Sound envelopes for bond formation chimes
Event permanence: crater tiles, waterlogged terrain

title: Visible motes and bonds
reflection: Motes grew from 1px to 5px crosses, bonds visible at alpha 100, spatial hash grid wired up.

title: Phase-tinted sky
reflection: Sky now interpolates per-phase color tints: violet at genesis, amber at dissolution, indigo at silence.
looking_ahead: Mote age visibility: gold tinge for elders

title: Visual overhaul
reflection: Added breathing pulse, cluster glow, soul-rise death particles, eclipse stars, aurora curtains, narrative text.
looking_ahead: Creature memory: motes returning to meaningful places
Visible trails and history accumulating through the cycle
Sound evolution: phase transitions with musical moments

title: Architecture refactor
reflection: Split 922-line main.ts into 25 focused files with clean layered DAG. No circular deps.
looking_ahead: Creature memory and meaningful return behaviors
Sound evolution: phase transitions with musical moments

title: Death Made Dramatic
reflection: Rewrote death particles into four phases (flash, shards, spirit orb, ground echo lasting 7.5s) using the mote's actual temperament color instead of biome palette.
looking_ahead: Temperament movement distinction: wanderers leave longer trails, social motes pulse when bonded
Bond formation visual moment: a brief arc of light connecting two motes
Cluster identity: large clusters develop a shared color aura
Hardy motes: visual resilience cue on hostile terrain

title: Five Worlds, Five Voices
reflection: Each biome now has a distinct sonic identity: root pitch, reverb length, filter Q, scale vocabulary per phase, stereo panning per voice cluster, LFO for organic filter breathing, perfect-fifth bond chimes, and descending-glide death sound.
looking_ahead: Spawn sound: gentle arrival tone per mote

title: The Sky Learns to Tell Time
reflection: Full cycle-driven solar arc, biome-aware weather particles (desert sand, volcanic ash, tundra blue-white), biome fog identity, multi-frequency water shimmer, and depth-blended background mountains.
looking_ahead: Volcanic lava pools as terrain feature

title: The Cycle Learns to Tell Its Own Story
reflection: Rewrote narrative arc: 4 deterministic opening lines per phase, event anticipation window 25s before trigger, aftermath lines, per-event trigger timing, sustained migration direction, expanded cycle name vocabulary.
looking_ahead: Event cascade narrative for phase overlaps
Cluster identity names in narrative text

title: Cinematic Post-Processing
reflection: Added phase-specific color grading, rebuilt aurora with 5 overlapping layers, phase flash with identity colors, phase-varying vignette, event message backing, warm glowing cursor.

title: Ambient Beds, Phase Chords, Spawn Pings
reflection: Added continuous biome ambient beds (sine drones, filtered noise), phase transition chord signatures, spread cluster voices across scale degrees for natural chords, spawn ping per new mote.

title: Bond Arc, Wanderer Trails, Social Pulse
reflection: Wanderers now leave 3x brighter trails via trailScale. Bonded social motes show 5-point resonance ring pulsing in sync. Bond formation gets converging spark arc.

title: Biomes Made Real
reflection: Desert gets cacti instead of trees, volcanic gets rock spires, tundra gets dead trees. Volcanic water renders as animated lava. Added horizon glow band and tundra frost crystals.

title: Five New Emotional Beats
reflection: Added first-death dirge, bond fragmentation line, peak population acknowledgment, last-survivor naming, and event-phase cascade lines. Event messages now have 3 per-cycle variants.

title: Bond Break, Cluster Territory
reflection: Bond breaks scatter six colored shards with dark core hole. Clusters of 4+ pulse a dashed perimeter ring; 6+ gain centroid spokes. bondBreakFlash field added to Mote.

title: Screen-Space Bloom — Creatures as Light
reflection: Added a full screen-space bloom pass: threshold bright pixels (luma > 140), apply a separable radius-4 box blur with pre-allocated Uint8 buffers (no per-frame GC), additively blend the glow back. Mote eyes, elder crowns, bond sparks, death flashes, and celestial bodies now radiate soft halos — the world feels lit from within. Bloom strength varies by phase (0.20 silence → 0.62 complexity) and spikes to 0.78 during eclipse so mote eyes become true lanterns in the dark. Click pulse gets a warm radial energy burst that also blooms, and the terrarium frame gains a slow 9-second amber breath.
looking_ahead: WebGL compositing for real-time HDR tone-map and motion blur
Per-biome bloom tint (warm for volcanic, cool for tundra)
Phase-specific particle showers (genesis stardust, dissolution ash)

title: Bond Break, Breathing Beds, Spatial Voices
reflection: Four gaps closed in one pass: bond breaks now have a two-tone descending glide (triangle + sine, starting at a fifth and falling apart — the inverse of bond formation's ascending fifth chime). Ambient beds become phase-reactive: genesis at 30% volume, complexity at 100%, silence at 10%, so the full 5-minute arc is now audible in the texture layer, not just in triggered notes. Cluster voices gain true stereo panning from their centroid X position, spreading the ensemble across the field instead of collapsing it to center. Each ambient bed now has an LFO (biome-tuned: 0.06 Hz desert heat shimmer to 0.18 Hz volcanic pulse) slowly breathing its filter cutoff, making the texture feel alive rather than mechanical.
looking_ahead: Cluster fragmentation sound: when a cluster loses a member, a scatter of brief high pings
Energy-mapped voice brightness: high-energy motes trigger brighter filter cutoffs and shorter decays
Per-biome phase transition variants: the dissolution chord in volcanic should be more dissonant than temperate
Mote-count-aware master mix: scale voice gain inversely with total mote count so complexity doesn't over-saturate

title: Light That Knows the Time
reflection: Three gaps that made the world feel frozen in place are now closed. Water tiles reflect the phase-tinted sky — at golden hour the lakes glow amber, at genesis they hold a violet sheen, at silence they go silver-indigo. Surface terrain tiles receive a phase lighting pass: a warm amber overlay during dissolution that fades in and out with a sine curve, cool blue at dawn, deep indigo moonlight at dusk. Desert air above the terrain gets a heat haze color shimmer — two overlapping noise frequencies give a warm yellow ripple that rises and fades with height, peaking at midday and dying at sunrise/sunset. Storm clouds gain a vertical gradient: near-black belly, grey-white top — immediately readable as threatening. Together these make every biome legible as a time-of-day experience: the world feels lit, not just drawn.
looking_ahead: Phase-reactive water level: flood event raises the water line progressively as it plays out
Volcanic ash accumulation overlay: during dissolution, ash tint builds on rocky ground tiles
Rain puddles: small water patches form on flat ground during rain, persist for a few seconds
Tundra aurora: the northern lights effect (currently eclipse-only) should trigger in tundra fog/clear nights

title: The World Learns Its Own Name
reflection: Four gaps closed in one pass. Every biome now narrates its own story: a volcanic genesis opens with 'fire touches the first light', a desert dissolution whispers 'the sand was always here' — the same arc, but in a different voice each cycle. A new milestone fires when the first cluster of three forms during organization: 'roots take hold' or 'the first hearth' — the moment a settlement becomes possible, now acknowledged. When all motes finally die, the world gets a final epitaph: 'gone, all of them' or 'what was here, was real'. Events now speak differently depending on where they happen: a bloom in volcanic reads 'life defies the ash', a drought in lush reads 'the green things wither'. And when weather and event collide at the same moment — storm+flood, fog+migration, snow+bloom — a second line queues right after the event start: 'storm and flood — the world drowns' or 'flowers push through the snow'. The silence phase gains two new lines ('what was here, was real', 'the echo of everything') and the names vocabulary grows by 24 adjectives and 10 features, adding words like MOLTEN, WHISPERING, TREMBLING, SACRED, and SORROW to the cycle-naming palette. Drought now dries up the edges of water tiles at onset — the lake visibly recedes, giving the event a terrain scar like flood and meteor already have.
looking_ahead: Cluster identity persistence: when the same group stays together for 30s+, give them a generated name that appears in narrative ('the RED BASIN cluster endures')
Phase-specific silence epitaphs that vary by what happened — if a plague ran, silence reads differently than if a bloom ended the cycle
Biome-aware aftermath: drought aftermath in desert vs tundra should feel different
A 'near miss' narrative moment: if a cluster almost reaches 6 but dissolves first, acknowledge the loss

title: Hardy Shape, Wanderer Lean
reflection: Hardy motes now have a visually distinct blocky silhouette — wide squared shoulders fill the normally-empty upper corners, making them immediately readable as stocky and resilient. When a hardy mote steps onto hostile terrain (lava, deep water, cliffs), silvery-blue corner sparks fire in a rapid pulse — a shield-flash that says 'taking damage, not dying'. Wanderers gain a forward lean: when wanderlust > 0.65 and moving fast, the head and neck pixels shift one pixel into the movement direction, creating a running posture that's visible within seconds. All three temperaments now have a unique silhouette and behavioral signature.
looking_ahead: Wanderer trail fade: old trail points shift toward the terrain color, so high-wanderlust motes leave a ghost of where they've been
Social mote cluster merge: when two clusters become one (a new bond forms between cluster members), a brief color-blend flash at the bond site
Hardy elder: old hardy motes develop a second wider shoulder layer — they accumulate visual mass over their lifetime
Low-energy wanderer: a frantic fast-flicker as energy nears zero, vs social motes that dim evenly — different deaths for different characters
